rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this InfoAction (active), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Primary blocker: evidence did not clear the directional decision threshold. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal argues treasury governance must provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano growth and stability." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide mitigation evidence; Provide independent assurance. Residual uncertainty: DRep ratification support is below threshold; this is not treated as active opposition. Confidence context: score -0.05; confidence 48.2%.
rationale.summary_raw: Advisory model lean was -0.010 (display cap ±0.05) and had zero influence on the binding score: The proposal aligns with the doctrine preference for governance clarity by outlining a budget-centered process, but the assessment marks evidence as thin and several high-materiality problem statements are asserted without supporting evidence. Because info actions have real coordination effects and treasury stewardship plus evidence quality rank above social momentum, a small cautionary negative nudge is warranted.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Governance info action to introduce discussion ideas for reforming Cardano treasury governance, including strategic budgeting, dedicated entities, expert evaluation, and a proposed budget structure.
Recipient: Cardano governance community / DReps / ecosystem participants
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Public discussion of treasury governance reform ideas, Outline of guardrails for strategic direction, Proposed treasury governance voting sequence, Proposed thematic budget structure, Future refinements and possible constitutional/process amendments

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal claims current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and ecosystem-harming conflict.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal argues treasury governance must provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano growth and stability.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal claims treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by the governance system and the constitution.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal argues the NCL should not be treated as the overall budget because it is only a vague initial approximation and insufficient without a coherent budget process.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal recommends a dedicated investment budget containing strategic direction and a roadmap.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and ecosystem-harming conflict.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal argues treasury governance must provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano growth and stability." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states that Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.
